question.text: Under SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, what information must an intermediary upload on the SEBI-specified website at the end of each quarter?

answer.text: Financial statements and audit reports for the quarter
Number of investor grievances received, redressed, and those remaining unresolved beyond three months
Only the number of investor grievances received during the quarter
Details of all trades executed during the quarter

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The regulations require intermediaries to upload, at the end of each quarter of a Financial Year ending on 31st March, information about investor grievances received, redressed, and those remaining unresolved beyond three months of receipt. **The Trap:** Uploading only the number of grievances received is incomplete - the regulation requires a more comprehensive disclosure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Quarterly reporting requirements are tested in NISM Series III-B and Series V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Quarterly grievance reporting ensures transparency and helps SEBI monitor intermediary responsiveness to investor complaints.
